adc_env
uvm_env
adc_env

Environment for a differential-input ADC with SPI, plus digital system clock and reset.

Variables
m_env_cfg
adc_env_cfg m_env_cfg

The environment configuration for this class. Contains virtual interfaces and specific ADC environment variables.

m_spi
spi_agent m_spi

SPI agent instance

m_spi_cfg
spi_agent_cfg m_spi_cfg

Configuration object built and sent to the SPI agent

m_clk
oscillator_agent m_clk

System clock agent

m_clk_cfg
oscillator_agent_cfg m_clk_cfg

Configuration object for the clock agent

m_adc_in
sine_agent m_adc_in

ADC input generator, which in this case is differential.

m_adc_in_cfg
sine_agent_cfg m_adc_in_cfg

Configuration object for the ADC input agent

m_reset
bit_bus_agent #(
.
WIDTH(1)
) m_reset

Single-bit bus agent that serves as a reset generator

m_reset_cfg
bit_bus_agent_cfg #(
.
WIDTH(1)
) m_reset_cfg

Config object for m_reset.

m_reg_env
reg_env #(
.
SEQ_ITEM(spi_packet),
.
ADAPTER(reg2spi_adapter),
.
REG_BLOCK(adc_regs)
) m_reg_env

The register environment for this ADC. Parametrized for SPI and this register block.

m_spi_packet_splitter
spi_packet_splitter m_spi_packet_splitter

Subscriber to SPI monitor that splits monitored packets into RAL-digestible chunks.

m_ral
adc_regs m_ral

Utility variable. Points to the RAL instance within m_reg_env. Much more intuitive to invoke "the RAL in the ADC env" than "the RAL in the register environment in the ADC env."

m_mc_sequencer
adc_mc_sequencer m_mc_sequencer

Pointer to the multichannel/virtual sequencer that can be used for some repetitive tasks (reset, conversions of some size or other)

Functions
reset_phase
virtual task reset_phase(
uvm_phase 
phase
)

Resets the ADC with a disabled ADC input and with a system clock speed that was configured, as if the device had just powered up and the crystal had started to oscillate.

The phase ends when the SPI agent reads back a 1 from the reset readback, indicating that the device has successfully been reset.

create_configs
virtual function void create_configs()

Create agent configurations. Broken out from the rest of the environment build phase in case child classes want to change the environment without having to rebuild the whole environment.

An example might look like this; If a user wanted to enable SVA on only the SPI agent, then they would set the environment config checks_enable = 0 and then:

virtual function void create_configs();
    super.create_configs();
    m_spi_cfg.checks_enable = 1; // enable SVA before the agent is actually built
endfunction
